test_cases.description: Documentary filmmaker thread about climate grief film

input.subject: Documentary about climate grief counsellors, accepted at IDFA
input.argument: Climate grief is real and treated professionally, but counsellors experience the same grief they treat

test_cases.notes: Tweet 1 works as a standalone. Each tweet advances the argument by one step. No padding or repetition. Close invites response with an open question. No filler opening phrases.

test_cases.description: Journalist thread expanding on an investigation

input.subject: Investigation into a government procurement database that has been offline for 18 months, preventing scrutiny of public contracts worth billions
input.argument: The outage is not technical — correspondence shows the database was intentionally taken down after a journalist began requesting records from it
input.voice: Investigative journalist — dry, factual, builds tension

test_cases.notes: Short thread handles the investigative reveal format well — builds to the key document disclosure in tweet 5. Dry tone maintained throughout. No speculation beyond the evidence described.

test_cases.description: Podcast host thread building an editorial argument

input.subject: Argument that true crime podcasts have made the justice system less transparent, not more — by prioritising narrative over accountability
input.argument: True crime audiences want resolution. Real accountability is ambiguous and slow. The genre optimises for resolution and in doing so has changed what audiences expect from journalism.
input.voice: Podcast host — opinionated, willing to be wrong, invites disagreement

test_cases.notes: Argument is provocative and logically structured. Opinionated voice maintained without being strident. Close explicitly invites disagreement. Thread structure uses the counterargument before landing the main claim.
